jokes.text: He apparently used nothing but gold to assemble his circuitry...believing it to be a better conductor of human emotion.
jokes.setup: Introduction of eccentric inventor Russell Borchert
jokes.punchline: The pseudo-scientific belief that gold conducts emotions better
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist logic that sounds plausible but is obviously ridiculous. Good worldbuilding comedy.

jokes.text: It was a weird time. The Bionic Woman won an Emmy.
jokes.setup: Context about the strange 1970s era
jokes.punchline: Using The Bionic Woman's Emmy as evidence of how weird the times were
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery and economy. The specificity of The Bionic Woman makes it land.

jokes.text: Those people were on LSD.
jokes.setup: Some people said he hid money using a portal to another dimension
jokes.punchline: Dismissive explanation for the fantastical theory
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan dismissal. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: Except our story tracks logically. - And we're not talking over each other.
jokes.setup: Comparison to The Goonies
jokes.punchline: Claiming superiority over a beloved film in absurdly specific ways
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on adventure movie tropes. Self-aware without being obnoxious.

jokes.text: Everyone talks at the same time. Sorry. - Can you stop?
jokes.setup: Just claimed they don't talk over each other
jokes.punchline: Immediately prove themselves wrong by talking over each other
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback. The irony is perfectly executed and earns the laugh.

jokes.text: The entire school is shaped like a penis.
jokes.setup: Looking at blueprints of the school
jokes.punchline: Noticing the phallic shape of the campus layout
jokes.explanation: Lazy visual gag that relies on penis humor. Low-hanging fruit with minimal craft.

jokes.text: What the hell does your penis look like? Obviously a cluster of buildings.
jokes.setup: Previous character's observation about school layout
jokes.punchline: Defensive response suggesting their penis looks like clustered buildings
jokes.explanation: Escalates the penis joke with absurd defensive logic. Slight improvement on the original.

jokes.text: Let's have a laugh at the freak.
jokes.setup: Awkward exchange about penis-shaped buildings
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact suggestion to mock the person
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan cruelty. The casual cruelty delivered matter-of-factly works well.

jokes.text: Check it out. Look at the bottle held by the school's first...and least acceptable mascot, Big Chief Drunky.
jokes.setup: Looking for clues to hidden treasure
jokes.punchline: The horrible inappropriateness of the mascot name
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary disguised as treasure hunting. The name is perfectly awful.

jokes.text: The sparks may be spelling out the next clue.
jokes.setup: Electrical accident with sparks flying
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempt to find meaning in random electrical sparks
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic. Character's desperation to find clues leads to ridiculous interpretation.

jokes.text: It's pronounced 'aluminium.' See, he's fine.
jokes.setup: Duncan was just electrocuted and someone says his food will taste like aluminum
jokes.punchline: His only concern is pronunciation while proving he's okay
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. Duncan's pedantry even after electrocution is very British and very him.

jokes.text: Oh, look, it's Jeff Winger, fun police...here to pull over our smiles...because our mouths have tinted windows.
jokes.setup: Jeff being a killjoy about their treasure hunt
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor comparing smiles to cars with tinted windows
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor. The 'tinted windows' comparison is unexpected and perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: Married? Well, you're gonna need way more doves than this.
jokes.setup: Britta and Jeff announce they're getting married
jokes.punchline: Practical concern about insufficient doves for the wedding
jokes.explanation: Absurd practical concern. The specificity of 'way more doves' makes it land well.

jokes.text: What does this look like, an hour-long episode of The Office?
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting they acknowledge the marriage announcement
jokes.punchline: Reference to The Office's wedding episodes being longer format
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about TV format differences. Decent but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Who's got rope? - I'm offended by that question.
jokes.setup: Someone asks who has rope for their adventure
jokes.punchline: Another character takes offense at the implication they wouldn't have rope
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor. The indignation at being unprepared is well-executed.

jokes.text: Well, it's never the first one...so we can cancel this one out. - Come on. I guess now this is the first one, so it should be the next one.
jokes.setup: Trying to figure out which key opens the door
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about which key counts as 'first'
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist logic. The circular reasoning about keys is believably frustrating.

jokes.text: Don't worry. I've been drinking.
jokes.setup: Carl warns Richie to be careful with the door
jokes.punchline: Drinking presented as reassurance rather than concern
jokes.explanation: Classic reversal of expectation. Drinking as confidence booster rather than impairment.

jokes.text: Oh, hello. Would you like some tea? The tea is swell.
jokes.setup: Aggressive breaking down of door
jokes.punchline: Borchert's polite, cheerful greeting as if nothing happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect character introduction. The politeness contrasts beautifully with the violent entry.

jokes.text: Do you think they can hea...? I think that... Oh, come on. You know it's just as loud as...
jokes.setup: School board members trying to whisper while making noise
jokes.punchline: Their failed attempts at stealth while being obviously loud
jokes.explanation: Standard bumbling authority figure comedy. Functional but not inspired.

jokes.text: The debate team topic is: 'Who's hotter, Elliott Gould or Donald Sutherland?' - Donald Sutherland. - Donald Sutherland.
jokes.setup: Finding evidence they're in the lost 1970s section
jokes.punchline: Hilariously dated debate topic with immediate unanimous agreement
jokes.explanation: Great period-specific humor. The immediate agreement makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: If it's like a regular dungeon, it's only as dangerous...as whoever invited you.
jokes.setup: Someone expressing concern about danger in the underground area
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to sex dungeons masquerading as adventure wisdom
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre. Works on both adventure and sexual levels simultaneously.

jokes.text: Won't know them from ordinary robots until they kill us.
jokes.setup: Asked to identify killer robots if they encounter them
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the logical flaw in the identification method
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed logic. Deadpan delivery of grimly practical observation.

jokes.text: This decade's out of my wheelhouse. Pet Rock, leave no Pet Rock unturned.
jokes.setup: Trying to search for 1970s clues but admitting ignorance of the era
jokes.punchline: Mangling the phrase while using the most stereotypical 70s reference
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with character-appropriate confusion about decades.

jokes.text: You're that insecure that your light has to be better? - Shut up. - You shut up.
jokes.setup: Someone insisting on leading because their light is better
jokes.punchline: Immediate defensive escalation into childish argument
jokes.explanation: Standard bickering comedy. Gets the job done but nothing special.

jokes.text: Don't cry. The importance of lumbar support hadn't... It's not that.
jokes.setup: Someone apparently crying at the sight of old beanbag chairs
jokes.punchline: Misreading the emotional reaction as being about furniture ergonomics
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection. The practical concern about furniture vs emotional response works well.

jokes.text: But I know TV. When characters feel the show they're on is ending...their instinct is to spin off into something safer.
jokes.setup: Annie expressing concern about the group falling apart
jokes.punchline: Abed analyzing their situation as TV characters facing cancellation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary. Abed perfectly analyzing the show's own situation through TV tropes.

jokes.text: In Jeff and Britta's case...something that'd last six episodes, have bickering...starring them and an equally WASP-y couple...with a title like 'Better With My Worse Half' or 'Tying the Not.'
jokes.setup: Continuing analysis of Jeff and Britta's potential spinoff
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and terrible sitcom title suggestions
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the meta joke with perfectly awful sitcom titles.

jokes.text: 'Knot' is spelled without a K. Or #CouplePeopleProblems.
jokes.setup: Previous sitcom title suggestions
jokes.punchline: Pedantic correction followed by hashtag-era sitcom title
jokes.explanation: Good continuation. The hashtag title feels authentically modern and terrible.

jokes.text: This is our show, and it's not over.
jokes.setup: Extended meta-commentary about the show ending
jokes.punchline: Direct address to the audience about the show's continuation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment. Breaking the fourth wall while staying in character.

jokes.text: I have a girlfriend. - What? - You were about to start a kiss lean. - I was not.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment building between Abed and Annie
jokes.punchline: Abed's awkward preemptive deflection of romantic tension
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific awkwardness. Abed's social awkwardness handled perfectly.

jokes.text: We've searched every crevice of this place...and the only treasures we've found are this CB radio...these basketball cards with white people on them...and this little rocket launcher from some action figure.
jokes.setup: Cataloging their disappointing treasure hunt findings
jokes.punchline: Escalating from mundane to oddly specific to randomly dangerous items
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from boring to weird. The 'white people' detail and tiny weapon work well.

jokes.text: Right. Because 127 in binary code would be a palindrome.
jokes.setup: Someone suggests trying track 127 on the jukebox
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong technical explanation delivered with confidence
jokes.explanation: Good pseudo-intellectual nonsense. The confident delivery of wrong information works.

jokes.text: Because the song title is 'Open the Door' by the Secret Doors.
jokes.setup: Previous wrong technical explanation for trying track 127
jokes.punchline: Actual simple reason that makes perfect sense
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection payoff. The simple explanation after the complicated nonsense lands well.

jokes.text: Okay, I'm gonna peel off some Band-Aids here. Dennis Hopper is dead. TV shows give no time for theme songs anymore. And Woody Allen did the voice of a cartoon ant.
jokes.setup: Breaking bad news to someone who's been underground since the 1970s
jokes.punchline: Escalating cultural disappointments ending with Woody Allen's career low
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of cultural disappointments. The Woody Allen detail is the perfect capstone.

jokes.text: Sorry, I lose track of how big I'm getting.
jokes.setup: Jeff apparently growing larger somehow
jokes.punchline: Casual apology for uncontrolled size increase
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual comedy. The casual delivery of impossible situation works well.

jokes.text: I don't.
jokes.setup: Jeff saying he loses track of how big he's getting
jokes.punchline: Someone else confirming they're keeping very close track
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response. Two words that land perfectly as a reaction.

jokes.text: Hot Lava. - That was Hot Lava? Dumb.
jokes.setup: Reference to previous game the study group played
jokes.punchline: Immediate dismissal after learning what it was called
jokes.explanation: Simple callback with good deadpan dismissal. Economy makes it work.

jokes.text: You're special, Richie. Your mind is special. You know it works differently from other kids' minds.
jokes.setup: Chang apparently using some kind of mind control on Richie
jokes.punchline: Patronizing language typically used for developmentally disabled children
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about manipulation. The patronizing tone is effectively uncomfortable.

jokes.text: They climbed out to the roof, hopped on a hang glider...and flew it across town to that Greek restaurant. Papa Cristo's?
jokes.setup: Richie being asked where the others went
jokes.punchline: Completely absurd escape plan with oddly specific restaurant destination
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation. The specific restaurant detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I robbed your brain. I robbed it.
jokes.setup: Chang successfully extracting information from Richie
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration about brain robbery with repetition for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good Chang character moment. The dramatic repetition is perfectly Chang-like.

jokes.text: Was there a hang glider? - You're gonna find a hang glider down there. That much I know.
jokes.setup: Carl asking practical questions about the escape story
jokes.punchline: Chang's confident prediction about finding impossible equipment
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the absurd hang glider bit. Chang's confidence sells it.

jokes.text: The '80s happened down here too.
jokes.setup: Previous revelation about running out of cocaine quickly
jokes.punchline: Explaining that even underground isolation couldn't prevent the 1980s
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line. Perfect explanation for cocaine excess with cultural commentary.

jokes.text: I don't think it's worth much. Those are Gerald Ford dollars.
jokes.setup: Offering his leftover money to help save Greendale
jokes.punchline: Treating currency as if it changes value based on which president was in office
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist economics. The specificity of Gerald Ford makes it work.

jokes.text: Back in the '70s, that was worth millions.
jokes.setup: Previous comment about Gerald Ford dollars not being worth much
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about money being worth what it was worth
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the currency confusion. Character logic stays consistent.

jokes.text: Get back. This pickle's a magnet. - I'll wipe her out, man.
jokes.setup: Chang threatening the computer with some kind of weapon
jokes.punchline: Describing weapon as a 'pickle' and treating it as dangerous to computers
jokes.explanation: Absurd weapon naming with genuine threat. 'Pickle' as magnet is perfectly weird.

jokes.text: Wait, I'm not wearing a mask? - There's no more to it. I think I'm just mentally ill.
jokes.setup: Chang checking for mask after previous dramatic statement
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation to simple mental illness explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang moment. Self-aware about his mental state while being completely unhinged.

jokes.text: Right there. That was it, that was it. Did you see it?
jokes.setup: Borchert showing off his computer that responds to feelings
jokes.punchline: Excited about barely perceptible response that nobody else can see
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. The overexcitement about nothing visible is believably pathetic.

jokes.text: And what's that? - An emoticon. That person wants to indicate happiness...but can't convey it with language, so they misuse punctuation to form a smile.
jokes.setup: Borchert seeing modern internet culture for the first time
jokes.punchline: Perfectly accurate but dismissive description of emoticons
jokes.explanation: Perfect outsider perspective on familiar technology. The clinical description makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That is so... - Stupid. Only an idiot would think of this. Idiots won!
jokes.setup: Borchert seeing evidence that his fears about idiots taking over came true
jokes.punchline: Dramatic realization that idiots have indeed won the cultural war
jokes.explanation: Perfect character arc completion. His fears realized in the most mundane way possible.

jokes.text: But that's only if somebody in this room...has feelings stronger than I get when I rub my nipples...so good luck with that, folks.
jokes.setup: Explaining how to restart the computer with human emotion
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about his nipple stimulation as the emotional baseline
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy. The oversharing combined with dismissive attitude works well.

jokes.text: I understand there's some Internet...where I can make my inner thoughts public.
jokes.setup: Borchert being introduced as new vice dean
jokes.punchline: His quaint 1970s understanding of social media as novel concept
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency. His naive excitement about internet culture.

jokes.text: We are but simple sandwich artists...who want the world to eat fresh. - Eat fresh!
jokes.setup: Subway retreating from the complicated school situation
jokes.punchline: Corporate speak followed by synchronized slogan chanting
jokes.explanation: Standard corporate satire. The synchronized chanting is effectively robotic.

jokes.text: Yeah? Well, around here, we call that Wednesday.
jokes.setup: Being told Greendale is still on the chopping block
jokes.punchline: Dismissive response suggesting constant crisis is normal
jokes.explanation: Good comeback line. Perfect encapsulation of Greendale's perpetual crisis state.

jokes.text: Doesn't electrocuted technically mean killed? - You always have to correct people? - You always have to police correcting?
jokes.setup: Someone saying Duncan was electrocuted earlier
jokes.punchline: Duncan's pedantry about terminology followed by meta-argument about correcting
jokes.explanation: Good character callback. Duncan's pedantry escalating into argument about pedantry itself.

jokes.text: I was thinking about a hang glider.
jokes.setup: Earlier absurd story about escaping via hang glider
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that he was actually thinking about hang gliders
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff. Reveals the absurd story had a mundane explanation.

jokes.text: None of this conflict truly matters. The only owners of this land are the Arapaho.
jokes.setup: All the legal maneuvering over Greendale ownership
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the fundamental illegitimacy of all claims due to Native American displacement
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary cutting through the plot. Unexpected perspective shift.

jokes.text: Hi, yes, I'd like to replace all my teeth with diamonds.
jokes.setup: Chang getting a job on the school board
jokes.punchline: Immediately using new position for ridiculous personal request
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang moment. Immediate corruption of authority for absurd personal gain.

jokes.text: We'll definitely be back next year. If not, it'll be because an asteroid has destroyed all human civilisation...and that's canon.
jokes.setup: Casual comment about returning next year
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and dramatic reasons for potential cancellation ending with 'canon' declaration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on show renewal anxiety. 'And that's canon' is perfect Abed.

jokes.text: Depends on what fails.
jokes.setup: Final scene with cryptic TV show titles
jokes.punchline: Ominous response to question about future plans
jokes.explanation: Good ominous closer. Economy and delivery make it work as final note.
